chief_complaint: A 35-year-old man presented with 3 days of epigastric abdominal pain.
history: A 35-year-old man with no known history of diabetes mellitus presented with 3 days of epigastric abdominal pain. He was treated with intravenous fluids, insulin infusion, and electrolyte replacement under a DKA protocol. He was placed on a fasting regimen (nil per os) and was monitored with serial metabolic panels and lipid assessments.
exam: Vital signs: not available. Physical examination: epigastric abdominal pain.
labs: Initial laboratory tests: DKA, severe hypertriglyceridemia (triglycerides 2464 mg/dL), no lipase elevation, no evidence of gallstones or alcohol-related pancreatitis.
correct_diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) complicated by hypertriglyceridemia-induced mild pancreatitis
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) complicated by hypertriglyceridemia-induced mild pancreatitis
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) complicated by hypertriglyceridemia-induced mild pancreatitis
